topic: Windsurf vs Cursor for Solo Developers in 2026

key_points: Both now cost $20/month at Pro level — decision is features, not price
Windsurf better for autonomous/agentic tasks and large refactors
Cursor better for community support, controlled diff-and-approve workflow
Windsurf free tier is genuinely useful for many solo devs to stay on indefinitely
Tested both tools head-to-head on real projects
Cursor needed 2 rounds for CSS table component vs Windsurf's 3 rounds
Windsurf's Cascade excelled at 3,000-line Express.js CommonJS→ESM migration with only 2 test failures out of 47
Cursor requires manual adjustments in complex production refactors
Windsurf offers plugins for 40+ IDEs vs Cursor's VS Code-only fork
Windsurf's SWE-1.5 processes 950 tokens/second — 13x faster than Claude Sonnet 4.5
Cursor switched to credit-based billing in June 2025
Windsurf switched to quota-based billing (daily/weekly) on March 19, 2026
Ran 7 real-world tests to determine a clear winner
Cursor's diff-and-approve loop is a feature for production safety, not a limitation
Cascade's autonomy is faster on greenfield work
Cursor's 20x scaled reinforcement learning reduces latency by 60%
Cursor dominates on ecosystem and community with 2M+ users
Windsurf bets on IDE freedom, enterprise compliance, and proprietary models
Background Agents in Cursor allow up to 8 parallel agents working simultaneously
Windsurf's Codemaps — AI-annotated visual code navigation — has no competitor equivalent

stats.fact: Cursor has 2M+ users, 1M+ paying customers, and $2B annualized revenue as of February 2026
Windsurf has 1M+ active users and was acquired by Cognition AI for ~$250M in December 2025
JetBrains Jan 2026 survey: GitHub Copilot 29%, Cursor 18% (tied with Claude Code), Windsurf 8% workplace adoption
AI coding tools market projected to grow from $5.5B in 2024 to $47.3B by 2034
91% of engineering organizations have adopted at least one AI coding tool
Windsurf's SWE-1.5 runs at 950 tokens/second — 13x faster than Claude Sonnet 4.5 and 6x faster than Haiku 4.5
Windsurf raised price from $15/month to $20/month on March 19, 2026, matching Cursor Pro pricing
Cursor 3.0 supports up to 8 parallel background agents with cloud-based execution
Windsurf reports 70M+ lines of code written by AI every day and 59% of Fortune 500 companies building with it
Windsurf had $82M ARR and 350+ enterprise customers at time of Cognition acquisition

angles: Price parity changed the game: Windsurf raised to $20/month in March 2026, making the choice purely about workflow preference
Cascade vs. Composer: autonomous driving vs. co-pilot — what solo devs actually want
Windsurf's 40+ IDE support vs Cursor's VS Code-only approach: flexibility for polyglot solo devs
SWE-1.5 model speed advantage (13x faster inference) gives Windsurf a real-time edge for rapid iteration
Cursor's massive community (2M+ users) means better StackOverflow coverage, more plugins, faster bug fixes
Free tier wars: Windsurf's free tier is genuinely production-capable vs Cursor's more limited free offering
For indie hackers: greenfield project agility (Windsurf) vs production codebase safety (Cursor)
Cognition's acquisition of Windsurf brings Devin-level agentic DNA to the IDE — underrated long-term advantage
